questions.question: What was the landmark in the image original main function?
questions.expected: The Brandenburg Gate originally served as a ceremonial city gate and entrance to Berlin
questions.rgb_answer: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch, intended to celebrate the victory of King Frederick William I of Prussia over the French in 1760. It was designed by architect Georg Wenzeslaus von Knobelsdorff and sculpted by Johann Gottfried Schadow. The gate was part of the city wall that surrounded Berlin and was meant to commemorate the Prussian victory and the unity of the German states under Prussian leadership. Over time, it became a symbol of Berlin and Germany, and after World War II, it was used as a border crossing between East and West Berlin. Today, it is one of Berlin's most famous landmarks and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
